items.title: Can NATO survive Trump's changes of heart?
items.description: These days, every official visit, every summit that involves the President of the United States seems like a mad scramble to keep the leader of the Atlantic Alliance on board. But what if it’s just too late? Last month, leaders left France thanking their lucky stars that Donald Trump hadn’t taken a big bazooka to the G7 Summit.In Ankara, a darker Donald threatening to pull all US troops from Europe, cut trade with Spain and blasting Nato for its unwillingness to join his war against Iran. He claimed he wouldn’t have even shown up but for his friend Recep Tayep Erdogan hosting. Putting put-downs and popularity contests aside, real lives are at stake. How do allies go faster in providing defence systems against Russia's cruise missiles lobbed on Ukraine? What if Vladimir Putin, whose citizens are feeling the pinch from Ukrainian drones targeting power and fuel supplies, decides to go for broke and test the resolve of frontline European states?

items.title: Erling #Haaland: Norway's record-breaking #WorldCup hero
items.description: He is helping #Norway perform at its best at the #WorldCup, and is considered one of the best goalscorers in the world. Who is Erling #Haaland?

items.title: Kenya 'Birdman' is internet hit but worries health officials
items.description: A formerly homeless man in Nairobi has become a viral sensation after years of rescuing and living with injured birds, but authorities are worried about disease risks.

items.title: German doctor jailed for murder of 15 patients and suspected of more
items.description: The German palliative care doctor has been sentenced to life imprisonment for killing 15 of his patients.

items.title: Arizona toddler found alive in hospital morgue after declared dead from drowning
items.description: After he was discovered to still be alive, the 18-month-old was flown to another hospital and survived.

items.title: Argentina survive Cape Verde scare to reach World Cup last 16
items.description: Cristian Romero's header in injury time secures 3-2 victory over Cape Verde in extra time; African side impressed in hard-fought contest in Miami, but Argentina's resilience and never-say-die attitude saw them through. Leer más

items.title: 'Not a handout': Australian theatre calls for government lifeline amid show closures
items.description: As two more major musicals bite the dust, the local theatre industry hopes a live performance incentive modelled on a successful UK scheme will provide salvation.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Australia dock workers call for 28-hour week in AI talks
items.description: A union says workers are "in the crosshairs" of automation as AI is being tested across ports.

items.title: India supports two-state solution for solving Israel-Palestine conflict, says PM Modi
items.description: In a press statement in Jakarta while presiding over the signing of multiple agreements, including the delivery of BrahMos supersonic cruise missile system and Astra Mk-1 beyond the visual-range air-to-air missiles to Indonesia, Mr. Modi says India believes that the role of dialogue and diplomacy has become more important than ever before

items.title: The state of the NATO alliance amid Trump's second term
items.description: Carl Bildt, co-chair of the European Council on Foreign Relations, talks about the ongoing NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, and the state of the alliance during President Trump's second term.

items.title: Mbappe hits back at racist insults from Paraguay senator
items.description: A Paraguayan lawmaker has sparked outrage with racist insults directed at footballer Kylian Mbappe. The French FA says it will take legal action, and President Emmanuel Macron has expressed support for the player.

items.title: Rahul Mishra brings ‘Devi’ to Paris Haute Couture Week 2026
items.description: Isha Ambani, Cardi B attend Paris Haute Couture Week

items.title: NRL admits bunker error in awarding crucial Blues try in Origin decider
items.description: The NRL admits the bunker missed a NSW knock-on in the lead-up to a crucial try that helped the Blues win Wednesday night's State of Origin decider at Lang Park.

items.title: East German doping victims still lack adequate support
items.description: Thousands of athletes in former East Germany were subjected to state-sponsored doping — and many still suffer lasting health effects. But support for victims is not enough, German commissioner says.

items.title: Central Bank survey sees inflation dipping below 2% from August
items.description: REM market expectations survey of consultancy firms, economists and experts forecasts 2026 annual inflation of 30%, with peso trading at 1,673 per US dollar by year-end. Leer más

items.title: Emmanuel Macron visits Syria, 18 months after Assad's fall
items.description: The French president landed in Damascus on Monday evening. He's the first EU leader to visit the country since Bashar Assad's sudden downfall and the rise of former militant turned President Ahmed al-Sharaa.

items.title: Dangote-backed mega oil refinery to be built in Kenya
items.description: A 700,000-bpd east African oil refinery proposed by Africa's richest man, Aliko Dangote, will be built in Kenya, a senior company official said Tuesday, putting a lid on speculation over the location of the mega-project.

items.title: Mass cleanup underway after severe flooding in China
items.description: Residents and volunteers in China’s Guangxi province have begun clearing up their flooded homes.

items.title: Russian fuel shortages bite – but will Putin change tack in Ukraine war?
items.description: Even in Moscow, authorities are unable to guarantee fuel supplies, but will economic pressure lead to talks or escalation?

items.title: Ukraine says its drones struck three refineries and tankers in Russia
items.description: Russia bans diesel exports to ensure domestic supply till July 31

items.title: Lawmakers ask Nigerian govt to halt rehabilitation of repentant terrorists
items.description: The lawmakers said the policy undermines efforts to combat insecurity and emboldens criminal elements. The post Lawmakers ask Nigerian govt to halt rehabilitation of repentant terrorists appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Trains and emergency calls affected after major outage at Australia's largest telecoms company
items.description: Servers at data centres in Sydney and Melbourne were to blame but the exact cause remains unknown.

items.title: Russian strikes kill at least 26 in Kyiv region on eve of NATO summit
items.description: The attack hit just days after another Russian strike killed more than 30 people in the Ukrainian capital

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: Milei plans to attend inaugurations of new Peru and Colombia presidents
items.description: President will travel to Lima and Bogotá for inauguration ceremonies; Milei welcomes election victories of Keiko Fujimori and Abelardo de la Espriella as signs of a broader right-ward shift in Latin America. Leer más

items.title: Kenya crushes Saba Saba march in latest crackdown on dissent
items.description: Kenyan police blocked a planned Saba Saba Day march in Nairobi on Tuesday, detaining protesters and sealing off the capital as President William Ruto's government intensified its crackdown on dissent amid lingering anger over police violence, economic hardship and shrinking civic freedoms.

items.title: Nigeria's president demands to know how fake agency was allegedly set up in his office
items.description: Adeniyi Adeyemi allegedly forged a letter of appointment and secured funding for an agency the presidency knew nothing about.

items.title: Lionel Messi versus Mohamed Salah in World Cup last-16 showdown
items.description: Argentina survived a huge scare from World Cup first-timers Cape Verde in the last 32. They will hope to resume normal service against Egypt – who have never been beyond the round of 16. Leer más

items.title: French icemakers under pressure as demand surges amid heatwave
items.description: Much of France is once again under severe heat alert and icemakers are under growing pressure to keep supplies up. But that's becoming increasingly difficult, leading to tough choices for some businesses.

items.title: Iran talks in doubts as Trump says ceasefire 'over'
items.description: US President Donald Trump said the ceasefire with Iran was "over" Wednesday but left the door open to more talks, after fighting flared sparked by Iranian attacks on ships in the vital Strait of Hormuz. "As far as I'm concerned, it's over," Trump said at a NATO summit in Turkey on Wednesday when asked if the truce was intact. "I'll let our wonderful negotiators keep talking if they want, but I don't see it. I don't like these people," he added.

items.title: Trump says he will allow Ukraine to make its own Patriot missiles
items.description: US President Donald Trump told his Ukrainian counterpart Volodymyr Zelensky on the sidelines of the NATO summit Wednesday that the US would grant Ukraine a license to manufacture its own Patriot air-defence systems. Patriot interceptor missiles are the only tool in Kyiv's arsenal that can shoot down Russia's deadly ballistic missiles.

items.title: ‘Down with the USA’: US influencer Jackson Hinkle attends Khamenei’s funera
items.description: ‘Down with the USA’: US influencer Jackson Hinkle attends Khamenei’s funeral

items.title: Outrage as FIFA's Infantino bends rules to Trump's whim at World Cup
items.description: FIFA boss slammed for controversial decision to suspend US star Folarin Balogun's one-game ban; Ruling, which came after the intervention of Donald Trump, permits striker to play in the co-hosts' World Cup last 16 match with Belgium. Leer más

items.title: UK's Farage resigns as MP to force snap vote in finances row
items.description: British anti-immigrant politician Nigel Farage announced Tuesday he would resign as a member of parliament to run in a snap by-election, in a high-stakes gamble following intense scrutiny over his finances. The unusual move comes as Farage, whose Reform UK party leads national opinion polls, faces mounting pressure over the non-disclosure of gifts, including allegedly from a convicted fraudster.

items.title: Hungary public media reform begins with black screen apology
items.description: People tuning in to public service media in Hungary on Tuesday were greeted by a black screen and an apology. Peter Magyar has begun dismantling one of Viktor Orban's most potent tools of power.

items.title: Former Ivory Coast defence minister released after 'terrorism' charges
items.description: Former Ivory Coast Defence Minister Moise Lida Kouassi has been released after spending nearly a year in detention on terrorism-related charges.

items.title: Israeli air strikes in Gaza kill eight, including two children
items.description: Medics report 12 injuries alongside eight deaths in Gaza as Israeli air strikes target civilians and displaced families.

items.title: No evidence via ‘Op. Hard Ball’ investigation links Indian Govt. to Nijjar killing: Canada Police
items.description: "There's no evidence to suggest through this organised crime investigation and the charges and the indictment laid forward that Indian officials were charged or involved," a senior Canadian police official said

items.title: From joy to despair: Egypt fans shocked by loss to Argentina
items.description: For football fans at this coffeehouse in Cairo, Tuesday’s World Cup match between Egypt and Argentina started with a bang. But then it all went wrong.

items.title: Ex-navy commander convicted over 2017 ARA San Juan submarine tragedy
items.description: Submarine force commander Claudio Villamide handed three-year suspended sentence for negligence and breach of duties; Three other Navy officers acquitted. Leer más

items.title: Trump’s Iran strike comments shake markets, Brent crude hits two-week high
items.description: Travel stocks plunged as fuel costs rise, United Airlines fell 3 percent, while energy stocks saw gains.

items.title: TV catchphrase quiz: Guess the show from the famous quote
items.description: Now for the story of 20 famous TV quotes, and the one quiz that had no choice but to keep them all together.

items.title: Dollar fades after touching one-week high as Iran deal in doubt
Andrés Gil Domínguez: ‘Eliminating the PASO primaries would strip opposition of tool to unify’
items.description: Constitutional lawyer analyses the possible elimination or suspension of the PASO primaries in Argentina, arguing that its possible reform or repeal responds more to political strategy than institutional improvement. Leer más
